parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06600-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Stamp & Shape Document

### Stamp & Shape Document

Shapes the item: writes `docId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.docId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Classify with AI · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Action Needed, Reference, Ignore (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Action Needed · ②→ Store as Reference, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Store as Ignore · ④→ Alert Slack — Other

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenAI Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Store as Action Needed

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Store as Reference

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Store as Ignore

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Alert Slack — Other

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (PostgreSQL)

### Append Audit Row (PostgreSQL)

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Distill documents with PostgreSQL and Slack end to end

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill documents with PostgreSQL and Slack end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual knowledge sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Stamp & Shape Document → Payload Valid? → Classify with AI → Store as Action Needed → Reunite Branches → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating knowledge sync in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: OpenAI API key; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks — the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your knowledge sync process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
